evidence: The paper states they determined "relative importance of phyla in classification accuracy per factor, as well as ASVs that contributed considerably to classifier accuracy (i.e., high gini importance)" and reports: "Proteobacteria was the most important phylum making between 39.7 and 54.6% of the relative importance to the classifier and Actinobacteria was the second most important phylum with between 14 and 24.9% of the relative importance." However, the paper's stated conclusions focus on compartment-specific microbiota dynamics and site effects on root versus leaf/berry communities, all corroborated by PERMANOVA, linear models on phyla abundance, and PCoA analyses - not by the importance rankings.
